title: Task listing + lifecycle transitions (start / done / drop)
description: Add `bober task list` and the status-transition subcommands `start`, `done`, and `drop`. Transitions read the active Finding by id, write a new value with the new status through the reconcile UPDATE path so the prior status is preserved as history. The default list shows only open/in-progress tasks; `--all` and `--status <s>` widen the view.

successCriteria.description: `npm run build` exits 0 with the list/start/done/drop subcommands and a transitionFinding helper added.

A unit test captures a task, runs the done transition, then reads the pool and asserts the active row for that id has status='done' while a superseded (t_invalidated set) historical row with status='open' still exists in the store.

A unit test asserts the default list returns only findings whose status is open or in-progress, and that after marking one done it is absent from the default list but present when status='done'/--all is requested.
A unit test runs the start transition on an open task and asserts the active row's status becomes 'in-progress'.
A unit test passes an unknown id to done/drop and asserts the handler sets exitCode=1 and returns without throwing.

nonGoals: Do NOT implement snooze in this sprint (sprint 3).
Do NOT delete or hard-remove rows — drop transitions to status='dropped' via supersede, never DELETE.
Do NOT change captureTask or the persistence convention from sprint 1.
Do NOT read the clock inside the transition helper — now is injected from the handler boundary.
stopConditions: `npm run build` exits 0 and all list/start/done/drop unit tests pass.
`bober task add` then `bober task done <id>` removes the task from the default `bober task list`, while `bober task list --all` still shows it with status=done.
definitionOfDone: A user can see their active tasks with `bober task list` (open + in-progress only), move a task through `bober task start <id>`, finish it with `bober task done <id>`, or abandon it with `bober task drop <id>`. Each transition supersedes the prior Finding row and inserts the new one so the earlier status survives as queryable history, and the default list hides terminal (done/dropped) tasks while `--all`/`--status` reveal them. All handlers exit 1 with a stderr message (never throwing) on bad input.
assumptions: transitionFinding(store, id, newStatus, {now, mutate?}) reads the active Finding for that id, applies the new status (and any field mutation), and writes it back via writeFact so reconcileFact UPDATE supersedes the old row (reconcile.ts:70-78).
Terminal findings remain active rows carrying status done/dropped; the list filters by the status field rather than by invalidation.
generatorNotes: Add a transitionFinding helper to src/hub/finding-store.ts (or task-inbox.ts): const f = readFindings(store).find(x => x.id === id); if absent return null; write {...f, status:newStatus, ...mutate} via writeFinding — because subject=id and predicate='finding' are unchanged but value differs, writeFact/reconcileFact takes the UPDATE branch (supersede old + insert new), preserving history. Add subcommands to src/cli/commands/task.ts: `list` (options --all, --status <s>; default filter status in {open,in-progress}; print a table mirroring facts list at facts.ts:146-205), `start <id>` (->in-progress), `done <id>` (->done), `drop <id>` (->dropped). Stamp now=new Date().toISOString() at each handler boundary. On a missing id, print a chalk.yellow not-found message, set exitCode=1, return.
evaluatorNotes: Verify done/drop do NOT call invalidateFact-only or DELETE — they must go through the UPDATE (supersede+insert) path so a historical row remains. Inspect the store after a done transition: there must be one active row (status=done) and at least one invalidated row (status=open) for the same id. Confirm the default list query excludes done/dropped and that --all includes them. Confirm transition helpers take now as a parameter (no clock reads inside).
